title: 8 Best Herbal Creams for Dandruff and Scalp Issues

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Terpinen-4-ol helps to treat dandruff by reducing inflammation and fighting fungal infections that cause it, while also soothing and calming the scalp to promote healthy hair growth.
Cineole is good for dandruff because it has strong antifungal and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off the yeast that causes flaking skin.
Quercetin is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and flaking caused by dandruff.
Linalool is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking that can contribute to dandruff.
Catechins (also known as stinging nettle) are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itchiness on the scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil cream, is a popular remedy for dandruff. This is because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to control the growth of yeast on the scalp, which is a common cause of dandruff. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and itchiness. Tea tree oil in the cream helps to unclog pores and reduce flaking, making it easier to manage dandruff. When used regularly, Melaleuca alternifolia cream can help to reduce the symptoms of dandruff, leaving the scalp feeling clean and healthy. It can also be used to treat other scalp conditions, such as itchiness and irritation, making it a versatile and effective treatment option.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal creams for dandruff relief, start by infusing dried nettle leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1/4 cup of dried nettle leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the infusion process. After the infusion is complete, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a moisturizing base like shea butter or beeswax in a double boiler. Heat the mixture gently until the shea butter or beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Once cooled, transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in the fridge to prolong its shelf life. Apply a small amount of the cream to the affected scalp and massage it in to help soothe and calm dandruff symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for dandruff

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for dandruff because they help to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, allowing the skin to heal and preventing the flakes that cause dandruff from forming.
Aloin has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to reduce dandruff by soothing the scalp, preventing fungal growth, and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Saponins are good for dandruff because they have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe itchy scalps, reduce flaking, and prevent the growth of fungi that cause dandruff.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a natural remedy for dandruff. Dandruff is a skin condition that causes flakes to fall off the scalp. It can be caused by yeast growth, sensitivity to hair products, or dry skin. Aloe barbadensis cream has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp. It reduces redness and irritation, which can make dandruff worse. The cream also helps to balance the pH of the scalp, creating an environment that's not suitable for yeast growth. This reduces the number of flakes that fall off, giving the scalp a chance to heal. The cream also moisturizes the scalp, keeping it hydrated and preventing dryness that can lead to dandruff. As a result, Aloe barbadensis cream can help to reduce dandruff symptoms and promote a healthy scalp.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal cream that helps with dandruff, you need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you need fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, you'll need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a base cream like shea or cocoa butter. You'll also need a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. To start, combine the Urtica dioica leaves and carrier oil in the double boiler, and let it simmer for about 2 hours to extract the herb's medicinal properties into the oil. Once the oil has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids, and then mix it with the base cream in a bowl. Finally, whip the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk until it's smooth and creamy, and transfer it to a jar or tin to store in the fridge for up to 6 months. This cream can be applied directly to the scalp to soothe and calm dandruff, and to promote healthy hair growth.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for dandruff

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and soothe the scalp, thereby preventing flaking and promoting healthy hair growth.
Carnosic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the irritated scalp and reduce redness associated with dandruff, thereby promoting a healthy scalp and preventing further flaking.
Caffeic acid is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm itchy scalps, reducing flaking and irritation.
Luteolin is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and flaking caused by dandruff.

Polyphenols are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe the itchy scalp, reduce flaking, and promote healthy hair growth by fighting off fungal infections that can cause dandruff.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream, also known as rosemary cream, is a natural remedy for dandruff. It works well because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the fungus Malassezia that causes dandruff. Rosemary contains compounds like camphor and borneol, which have a soothing effect on the scalp and reduce inflammation. This helps to calm redness and itching associated with dandruff. Additionally, rosemary cream has a decongestant property that helps to unclog pores on the scalp, allowing for better skin cell regeneration and reducing flaking. Its astringent properties also help to balance the scalp's pH, preventing irritation and itchiness. The antioxidants present in rosemary cream protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and a dandruff-free scalp. By controlling the growth of fungus, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy scalp conditions, rosemary cream provides effective relief from dandruff symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal cream that helps with dandruff, you'll need dried nettle leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few basic kitchen tools. First, steep 1 tablespoon of dried nettle leaves in 2 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil. Next, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax over low heat, then slowly add the infused oil mixture to the melted wax, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and reaches a creamy consistency. Remove from heat and let cool slightly before transferring the mixture to a clean container. Once cooled and set, your Urtica dioica herbal cream is ready to use: simply apply it to your scalp, massage it in, and leave it on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing it off with warm water. This natural remedy can help soothe and calm dandruff symptoms, reducing flaking and itchiness.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for dandruff

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Limonene is good for dandruff because it helps to reduce inflammation and kill the fungus that causes dandruff, soothing and calming the scalp and promoting healthy hair growth.
Geraniol is good for dandruff because it has antifungal properties that help reduce the growth of yeast that causes flaking and itchiness on the scalp.
Linalools are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing flaking and itchiness associated with dandruff.
Citrals are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and flaking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus cream is a good treatment for dandruff because it has antifungal properties. This means it can fight off the fungus that causes dandruff. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to calm down the itchiness and redness that come with dandruff. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus cream has antiseptic properties, which helps to prevent infections and promote healthy scalp growth. This makes it a good treatment for dandruff, as it not only treats the symptoms but also helps to prevent future outbreaks.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal cream that helps with dandruff, start by gathering dried nettle leaves, which can be purchased at a health food store or grown in your garden. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of dried nettle leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a saucepan, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to release the herbal properties. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, and let it cool. Once cooled, add 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the bowl. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts, stirring occasionally. Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency similar to thick honey. Pour the mixture into a clean jar, and let it cool completely before using. To apply the cream, massage a small amount onto your scalp, focusing on areas affected by dandruff. Leave the cream on for 30 minutes to an hour before shampooing as usual. The anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of Urtica dioica will help soothe and calm your scalp, reducing dandruff and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams for dandruff

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for dandruff because it has antifungal properties that can help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, reducing inflammation and flaking associated with the condition.
Quercetin is good for dandruff because it helps reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, thereby preventing the flaking and itchiness associated with this skin condition.
Tannins are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking that can cause dandruff.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a good treatment for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce redness and itching on the scalp. The cream also has antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of yeast on the scalp that can cause dandruff. Eucalyptus globulus has a cooling effect, which can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce flaking. Additionally, the cream's astringent properties can help tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing the amount of oil and dirt that can contribute to dandruff. This natural ingredient is also gentle on the scalp and can be used regularly to help manage dandruff symptoms. Eucalyptus globulus cream can be applied directly to the affected area and massaged in to help relieve dandruff symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal creams that help with dandruff, start by gathering fresh or dried nettle leaves (Urtica dioica). If using fresh leaves, clean them thoroughly and dry them in a low-temperature oven or with a food dehydrator to preserve their medicinal properties. Then, mix the dried nettle leaves with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1 part nettle leaves to 10 parts carrier oil. Allow the mixture to infuse for several weeks, shaking the container occasionally, to extract the medicinal properties from the nettle leaves. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids. Finally, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, and add a few drops of an essential oil, like tea tree oil, to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory cream that can help to reduce dandruff and soothe an itchy scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for dandruff

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cinnamaldehyde is good for dandruff because it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, preventing flakes and itchiness associated with dandruff.
Tannins are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm itchy scalps, reducing flaking and promoting healthy hair growth.
Eugenol is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and flaking associated with dandruff.
Polyphenols are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the itchy, flaky scalp, reducing the symptoms of dandruff and promoting healthy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum cream is a natural remedy that can help with dandruff problems. The active ingredient, cinnamon oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and itching on the scalp. This oil also has antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff. Cinnamon oil is also a natural antiseptic, which means it can help kill bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to dandruff. When applied to the scalp, it can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing flaking and irritation. The cream also contains other natural ingredients, such as coconut oil and shea butter, which can help to moisturize and nourish the scalp, leaving it feeling soft and healthy. Regular use of Cinnamomum zeylanicum cream can lead to a reduction in dandruff symptoms, such as flaking and itching, and can promote a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal cream for dandruff, start by harvesting fresh or dried nettle leaves and flowers from the Urtica dioica plant. You can use a combination of both for better results. Rinse the nettle leaves and flowers in cold water, then chop them into small pieces. Combine the chopped nettle leaves and flowers with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is warm and fragrant. Let it cool down, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Discard the solids and transfer the nettle infused oil to a blender or a food processor. Add a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to the oil and blend until smooth. Finally, pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before applying it to your scalp to help soothe and calm dandruff.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum zeylanicum herbal creams for dandruff

Linalyl acetate is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and itchiness associated with dandruff.
Rosmarinic acid is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking caused by dandruff.
Caffeic acid is good for dandruff because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness associated with dandruff.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams, also known as lavender oil, can help with dandruff in several ways. The main active ingredient in lavender oil is linalool, a natural antifungal agent that fights off the fungus that causes dandruff. By reducing the fungus growth, the cream helps to stop the itching and flaking of the scalp. Lavender oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which calm the irritation and redness of the scalp. Additionally, the cream helps to balance the pH of the scalp, creating an environment that is not suitable for the fungus to grow. This, combined with the soothing and calming effects of lavender, can help to reduce the appearance of dandruff and leave the scalp feeling refreshed and clean.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal cream for dandruff relief, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Urtica dioica leaves, also known as nettle leaves. Then, mix 1 tablespoon of dried nettle leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a bowl, let it steep for 10-15 minutes to create a strong nettle tea. Strain the liquid and discard the leaves. Combine the nettle tea with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. As the ingredients melt, stir them well to create a smooth mixture. Remove the mixture from the heat and add 10 drops of tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties to help combat dandruff. Let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a jar. This Urtica dioica herbal cream can be applied directly to the scalp to help soothe and calm dandruff symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for dandruff

Tannins are good for dandruff because they have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing flaking and irritation.
Polyphenols are good for dandruff because they help reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, which can help to calm irritated skin and prevent the flaking that characterizes dandruff.

Saponins are good for dandruff because they gently cleanse and soothe the scalp, reducing flaking and inflammation while promoting a healthy balance of skin pH.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica cream is a natural remedy that can help with dandruff. The cream is made from the leaves of the plant Stinging Nettle (Urtica dioica), which has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce the redness and itching that often come with dandruff. The cream also has antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff. Urtica dioica cream can also help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking. Additionally, the cream has been shown to improve scalp health by reducing the buildup of dead skin cells, which can contribute to dandruff.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal cream for dandruff, you'll need fresh or dried nettle leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin E oil. Start by infusing the nettle leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, by placing the leaves in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it occasionally, until the oil has turned green and has a nettle-like scent. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with melted beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, add vitamin E oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely. Once set, your nettle cream is ready to use - simply apply it to your scalp and massage it in to help soothe and calm dandruff.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for dandruff

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great solution for people dealing with dandruff. Dandruff is a common scalp condition that causes flakes of dead skin to appear, leading to embarrassment and discomfort. If left untreated, it can lead to hair loss and even affect our self-confidence, making everyday life more challenging. To combat this issue, some herbal creams contain Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties. This helps to reduce the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff. Another key ingredient is Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, which soothes and calms the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin. By using a herbal cream that includes these ingredients, you can help to reduce the appearance of dandruff, preventing flakes from forming and keeping your scalp healthy. This can improve your life by boosting your self-esteem, making you feel more confident and comfortable in your own skin. By addressing this issue, you can also prevent potential long-term damage to your hair and scalp, ensuring they remain healthy and strong.
supplementary_best: For dandruff, the best combination of herbal creams is a blend of 'Melaleuca alternifolia' (tea tree oil), 'Urtica dioica' (nettle), and 'Aloe barbadensis' (aloe vera). Tea tree oil has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help fight the yeast that causes dandruff. Nettle is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the scalp and reduce redness. Aloe vera cools and moisturizes the scalp, preventing flaking and itching. This combination works well because tea tree oil targets the root cause of dandruff, while nettle and aloe vera calm and comfort the affected area. When used together, these three herbs create a powerful and gentle remedy that effectively treats dandruff without irritating the scalp.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dandruff, it's best to avoid creams with herbal ingredients like tea tree is not mentioned, but some herbal creams that can cause problems are those with Comfrey (Symphytum officinale), it can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. Another one to avoid is Burdock (Arctium lappa) as its root can cause skin problems and interact with medications. Also, Sassafras (Sassafras albidum) contains a compound that can cause skin irritation, especially if you have sensitive skin. Additionally, creams with Calendula (Calendula officinalis) should be used with caution as it can be too harsh for some people's skin. And, Witch Hazel (Hamamelis virginiana) can dry out the skin, making dandruff worse. Lastly, creams with Sage (Salvia officinalis) should be avoided as it can cause skin irritation and interact with medications.
supplementary_other_remedies: Apple cider vinegar is another natural remedy that can help with dandruff. It has antifungal properties that can help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, which can cause dandruff. To use apple cider vinegar, mix it with water and apply it to your scalp before washing your hair. The acidity in the vinegar helps to balance the pH of the scalp and reduce inflammation, which can help to soothe itchy skin and reduce flaking.  Another natural remedy for dandruff is coconut oil. Coconut oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and itching on the scalp. It also has antifungal properties that can help to control the growth of yeast and other fungi that can cause dandruff. To use coconut oil, massage it into your scalp and leave it on for a few hours before washing your hair.  Baking soda is also a natural remedy for dandruff. It has antifungal properties that can help to control the growth of yeast and other fungi that can cause dandruff. Baking soda can also help to balance the pH of the scalp and reduce inflammation. To use baking soda, mix it with water to make a paste and apply it to your scalp before washing your hair.  Olive oil is another natural remedy for dandruff. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and itching on the scalp. Olive oil can also help to moisturize the scalp and reduce flaking. To use olive oil, massage it into your scalp and leave it on for a few hours before washing your hair.  Tea tree oil is a natural antifungal agent that can help to control the growth of yeast and other fungi that can cause dandruff. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and itching on the scalp. To use tea tree oil, mix a few drops with your shampoo or apply it directly to your scalp before washing your hair.  Aloe vera is a natural remedy for dandruff that can help to soothe itchy skin and reduce flaking. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and itching on the scalp. Aloe vera can also help to moisturize the scalp and reduce flaking. To use aloe vera, apply it directly to your scalp before washing your hair.  These natural remedies can help to control the growth of yeast and other fungi that can cause dandruff, reduce inflammation, and soothe itchy skin. They can be used alone or in combination with each other to help manage dandruff.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dandruff often experience other skin problems, such as itchy scalp, redness, and flakes. These symptoms are usually caused by a fungal infection, such as Malassezia, which feeds on the oils produced by the scalp. To treat these conditions, medicinal herbs like neem and tea tree oil can be used. Neem has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce the growth of Malassezia and prevent further infection. Tea tree oil, on the other hand, has antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation.  Another common problem for people with dandruff is eczema, a condition characterized by dry, itchy skin. Aloe vera is a medicinal herb that can help to heal eczema by soothing and moisturizing the skin. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness and swelling.  Some people with dandruff may also experience hair loss, which can be caused by a variety of factors, including stress, poor diet, and hormonal imbalances. Saw palmetto is a medicinal herb that can help to promote hair growth by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hair loss.  Additionally, people with dandruff may experience digestive problems, such as bloating, gas, and diarrhea. These symptoms can be caused by an imbalance of gut bacteria, which can be treated with medicinal herbs like probiotics and turmeric. Probiotics help to restore the balance of gut bacteria, while turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the digestive system.  Finally, some people with dandruff may experience fatigue and low energy levels, which can be caused by an underlying nutritional deficiency or an imbalance of gut bacteria. Ginseng is a medicinal herb that can help to boost energy levels and improve overall well-being by stimulating the immune system and improving digestion.
